title: hieracium lachenalii subsp. tortifolium
intro: Hieracium lachenalii subsp. tortifolium, also known as creeping hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of creeping hawkweed include its ability to treat various skin conditions, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues, making it a valuable herbal remedy for promoting overall well-being. Creeping hawkweed can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its full potential and efficacy.
